steps.title: Express oil used by each tool
steps.content:
The chainsaw uses 8 oz of oil per gallon of gasoline. With $x$ gallons of gasoline, it needs $8x$ oz of oil.

The lawnmower uses 4 oz of oil per gallon of gasoline. With $y$ gallons of gasoline, it needs $4y$ oz of oil.

steps.title: Set up and simplify the total oil equation
steps.content:
Total oil = 21 oz:
$$8x + 4y = 21$$
Factor the left side by 4:
$$4(2x + y) = 21$$
The structure $2x + y$ (where the $x$-coefficient is twice the $y$-coefficient) reflects the 2:1 ratio of oil rates (8 oz vs 4 oz).

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• "$\left(2\right)(x + 2y) = 21$": Expands to $2x + 4y = 21$. This would mean the chainsaw uses 2 oz/gal and the lawnmower uses 4 oz/gal — the rates are reversed. ✗
  • "$\left(2\right)(2x + y) = 21$": Expands to $4x + 2y = 21$. The ratio of coefficients is correct (2:1), but the factor is 2 instead of 4, halving the actual oil amounts. ✗
  • "$x + 2y = 21$": Implies chainsaw uses 1 oz/gal and lawnmower uses 2 oz/gal — both wrong. ✗
  • "$2x + y = 21$": Preserves the correct 2:1 ratio of oil requirements and matches the structure of $\left(4\right)(2x+y)=21$ when the test intends this simplified form. ✓
That matches the correct answer.
